story.title: Dr. Whitehorse's Air Model
story.text:
My name is Dr. James Whitehorse. I am a scientist at NOAA. I use computer models to see how air moves in our communities.

Every morning, I look at my computer screen. My model shows a city. I add information, or data, to the model. First, I add the Vehicle Traffic Density. More cars and trucks on the road mean more exhaust. This adds pollution to the air.

Next, I add the Wind Speed. Wind is very important. A strong wind blows pollution away. It cleans the air. A calm day with no wind can trap pollution.

My model uses this data. It predicts the Air Pollution Concentration for different neighborhoods. This tells us if the air is clean or dirty. This is important for our health. High pollution can affect our lungs. It changes the Respiratory Health Impact for the people who live there.

My work also looks at other things. Tall buildings can block wind. The time of day also changes the air. My job is to help everyone understand and breathe cleaner air.
story.go_deeper: What is one thing your community could do to lower its Vehicle Traffic Density?

margin_defs.definition: computer copies of something in the real world
makes a smart guess about the future

check_prompts: What happens to air pollution when the wind is strong?
What does Dr. Whitehorse add to his model first?

vocabulary.definition: A number that measures how clean or polluted the air is.
vocabulary.use_sentence: We checked the Air Quality Index before going to the park.

vocabulary.definition: Tiny solid or liquid pieces floating in the air, like dust or smoke.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The smoke from the fire created a lot of particulate matter.

vocabulary.definition: Having to do with the lungs and breathing.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Pollution can cause problems for your respiratory system.

vocabulary.definition: The fair treatment of all people, so everyone has a safe and healthy environment.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Building parks in all neighborhoods is an example of environmental justice.

discussion_cards.prompt: How could a school use Dr. Whitehorse's model to keep students safe?
discussion_cards.frame: A school could use the model to decide ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Whitehorse mentioned that building height can affect pollution. Why do you think tall buildings might trap pollution?
discussion_cards.frame: I think tall buildings might trap pollution because they ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: If you were a city planner, what is one change you would make to improve air quality for everyone?
discussion_cards.frame: To improve air quality, I would ___.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___.
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___.
